label: Uncomplicated osteoarthritis flare without septic-joint or trauma concern
summary: Your symptoms fit an osteoarthritis flare.
found: Your clinician diagnosed an uncomplicated osteoarthritis flare. We did not find septic joint concern, trauma, neurovascular compromise, or another emergency problem today.
home: Rest from activities that worsen pain.
Use heat, ice, supports, or activity changes only as instructed.
Return to usual activity gradually as symptoms improve.
meds: Use pain medicine only if prescribed or recommended, and follow the label.
Avoid medicines your clinician told you to avoid.
return: Fever, red hot joint, spreading redness, or feeling very ill.
New injury, severe worsening pain, inability to use the limb, or inability to walk.
New numbness, weakness, cold/blue limb, or symptoms not improving.
follow: Follow up with primary care, rheumatology, sports medicine, or orthopedics if pain or function is not improving.
inclusion: Adult with clinician diagnosis or discharge impression of uncomplicated osteoarthritis flare.
No septic joint/infection concern, trauma/fracture concern, neurovascular compromise, inability to use limb, high-risk host, pregnancy/pediatric pathway, or specialist-directed plan.
exclusion: Septic joint concern, fever, red hot joint, cellulitis, open wound, fracture/dislocation concern, neurovascular compromise, inability to use limb, severe pain, or high-energy trauma.
Immunocompromised host, pregnancy/pediatric pathway, or specialist-directed orthopedic/rheumatology plan.
source_audit_notes: MedlinePlus supports osteoarthritis framing and supportive care. Autonomous Phase 1901-2000 approved this narrow v1 only for clinician-diagnosed uncomplicated osteoarthritis flare without septic joint/infection concern, trauma/fracture concern, neurovascular compromise, high-risk host, pregnancy/pediatric pathway, or specialist-directed plan.
required_modifiers: adult with clinician diagnosis or discharge impression of uncomplicated osteoarthritis flare
no septic joint/infection concern, trauma/fracture concern, or neurovascular red flags documented

label: Uncomplicated costochondritis without cardiopulmonary red flags
summary: Your symptoms fit costochondritis, irritation where the ribs meet the breastbone.
found: Your clinician diagnosed uncomplicated costochondritis. We did not find cardiac, lung, trauma, or other emergency red flags today.
home: Rest from activities that worsen the pain.
Use heat or ice only as instructed.
Return to activity gradually as symptoms improve.
meds: Use pain medicine only if prescribed or recommended, and follow the label.
Avoid medicines your clinician told you to avoid.
return: Pressure-like chest pain, pain with exertion, fainting, sweating, or pain spreading to the arm, jaw, or back.
Shortness of breath, coughing blood, fever, or feeling very ill.
New trauma, severe worsening pain, or symptoms not improving.
follow: Follow up with primary care if symptoms persist, recur, or your clinician told you to recheck.
inclusion: Adult with clinician diagnosis or discharge impression of uncomplicated costochondritis.
No cardiac/PE concern, unresolved dyspnea, hypoxia, hemoptysis, fever/systemic illness, trauma/fracture concern, pregnancy/pediatric pathway, or specialist-directed plan.
exclusion: Cardiac or PE concern, pressure/exertional chest pain, shortness of breath, hypoxia, hemoptysis, syncope, fever, systemic illness, trauma/fracture concern, or diagnostic uncertainty.
Pregnancy/pediatric pathway or specialist-directed cardiology/pulmonary plan.
source_audit_notes: MedlinePlus supports costochondritis framing and supportive care. Autonomous Phase 1901-2000 approved this narrow v1 only for clinician-diagnosed uncomplicated costochondritis without cardiac/PE concern, unresolved dyspnea, hypoxia, hemoptysis, syncope, systemic illness, trauma/fracture concern, pregnancy/pediatric pathway, or specialist-directed plan.
required_modifiers: adult with clinician diagnosis or discharge impression of uncomplicated costochondritis
no cardiac/PE concern, shortness of breath, hypoxia, hemoptysis, or trauma documented

label: Uncomplicated trigger finger without infection or neurovascular symptoms
summary: Your symptoms fit trigger finger, where a finger or thumb catches or locks with movement.
found: Your clinician diagnosed uncomplicated trigger finger. We did not find infection, tendon rupture, neurovascular compromise, trauma, or another emergency problem today.
home: Rest from gripping or repetitive activity that worsens symptoms.
Use a splint or activity changes only as instructed.
Do not force the finger if it locks painfully.
meds: Use pain medicine only if prescribed or recommended, and follow the label.
Avoid medicines your clinician told you to avoid.
return: New numbness, weakness, color change, severe pain, or inability to move the finger.
Fever, spreading redness, warmth, pus, or red streaks.
New injury, tendon rupture concern, or symptoms not improving.
follow: Follow up with primary care, sports medicine, orthopedics, or hand surgery if symptoms persist or function is limited.
inclusion: Adult with clinician diagnosis or discharge impression of uncomplicated trigger finger/thumb.
No infection, open wound, tendon rupture, neurovascular compromise, trauma/fracture concern, pregnancy/pediatric pathway, or specialist-directed plan.
exclusion: Infection, open wound, flexor tenosynovitis concern, tendon rupture, neurovascular compromise, fracture/dislocation concern, severe pain, or inability to move the finger.
Pregnancy/pediatric pathway or specialist-directed hand/orthopedic plan.
source_audit_notes: MedlinePlus supports trigger-finger framing and supportive care. Autonomous Phase 1901-2000 approved this narrow v1 only for clinician-diagnosed uncomplicated trigger finger/thumb without infection, open wound, tendon rupture, neurovascular compromise, trauma/fracture concern, pregnancy/pediatric pathway, or specialist-directed plan.
required_modifiers: adult with clinician diagnosis or discharge impression of uncomplicated trigger finger/thumb
no infection, tendon rupture, trauma/fracture concern, or neurovascular red flags documented

label: Minor nail injury without fracture, nail-bed laceration, or infection
summary: Your symptoms fit a minor nail injury.
found: Your clinician diagnosed a minor nail injury. We did not find open fracture, nail-bed laceration needing repair, infection, neurovascular compromise, or another emergency problem today.
home: Keep the nail area clean and protected.
Avoid picking, cutting, or drilling the nail at home.
Protect the finger or toe from repeat injury while it heals.
meds: Use pain medicine only if prescribed or recommended, and follow the label.
Take antibiotics only if your clinician prescribed them for a separate reason.
return: Severe worsening pain, numbness, color change, or inability to move the finger or toe.
Spreading redness, warmth, pus, red streaks, fever, or worsening swelling.
The nail lifts off, the wound opens, or there is new fracture concern.
follow: Follow up with primary care, urgent care, podiatry, or hand/orthopedic care if pain or nail healing is not improving.
inclusion: Adult with clinician diagnosis or discharge impression of minor nail injury/subungual bruising.

No open fracture, nail-bed laceration needing repair, crush injury emergency, infection, neurovascular compromise, diabetic-foot/PVD/neuropathy risk, pregnancy/pediatric pathway, or specialist-directed plan.
exclusion: Open fracture, fracture/dislocation concern, nail-bed laceration needing repair, severe crush injury, uncontrolled pain, infection, open wound with contamination, neurovascular compromise, or inability to use digit.
Diabetic foot risk, neuropathy, PVD, pregnancy/pediatric pathway, or specialist-directed hand/podiatry plan.
source_audit_notes: MedlinePlus supports minor nail-injury framing and home protection/cleanliness concepts. Autonomous Phase 1901-2000 approved this narrow v1 only for clinician-diagnosed minor nail injury/subungual bruising without open fracture, nail-bed laceration needing repair, infection, neurovascular compromise, diabetic-foot/PVD/neuropathy risk, pregnancy/pediatric pathway, or specialist-directed plan.
required_modifiers: adult with clinician diagnosis or discharge impression of minor nail injury/subungual bruising
no open fracture, infection, or neurovascular red flags documented

label: Uncomplicated TMJ pain without dental, deep-space, or neurologic red flags
summary: Your symptoms fit TMJ pain, irritation around the jaw joint.
found: Your clinician diagnosed uncomplicated TMJ pain. We did not find dental infection, deep-space infection, trauma, neurologic symptoms, or another emergency problem today.
home: Avoid hard chewing, gum, and wide jaw opening while symptoms are flaring.
Use heat, ice, or jaw rest only as instructed.
Eat softer foods for a short time if recommended.
meds: Use pain medicine only if prescribed or recommended, and follow the label.
Avoid medicines your clinician told you to avoid.
return: Face or neck swelling, fever, trouble swallowing, drooling, voice change, or trouble breathing.
Cannot open the mouth, new trauma, dental swelling, severe headache, or neurologic symptoms.
Chest pressure, fainting, or pain spreading to arm, jaw, or back.
follow: Follow up with primary care, dentist, oral surgery, or ENT if symptoms persist, recur, or your clinician told you to recheck.
inclusion: Adult with clinician diagnosis or discharge impression of uncomplicated TMJ pain/disorder.
No dental infection, deep-space infection, trismus, airway symptoms, trauma/fracture concern, neurologic deficit, cardiac chest pain mimic, pregnancy/pediatric pathway, or specialist-directed plan.
exclusion: Dental infection, deep-space swelling, trismus, airway symptoms, drooling, fever/systemic illness, facial trauma/fracture concern, neurologic deficit, severe headache, or cardiac chest-pain concern.
Pregnancy/pediatric pathway or specialist-directed ENT/dental/oral-surgery plan.
source_audit_notes: MedlinePlus supports TMJ-disorder framing and supportive care such as jaw rest and avoiding hard chewing. Autonomous Phase 1901-2000 approved this narrow v1 only for clinician-diagnosed uncomplicated TMJ pain/disorder without dental/deep-space infection, trismus, airway symptoms, trauma/fracture concern, neurologic deficit, cardiac mimic, pregnancy/pediatric pathway, or specialist-directed plan.
required_modifiers: adult with clinician diagnosis or discharge impression of uncomplicated TMJ pain/disorder
no dental infection, deep-space infection, trismus, neurologic deficit, trauma, or cardiac mimic documented
